name: Sonlight
description: Literature-rich Christian curriculum with comprehensive read-aloud-based lesson plans covering all subjects in one package
notes: All-Subjects Packages include history, Bible, geography, read-alouds, readers, language arts, math (Math-U-See default, swappable), and science. 4- or 5-day schedule options. Very reading-intensive — requires significant daily parent read-aloud time. Books are reusable for younger siblings. Love to Learn guarantee allows 18-week trial with full refund.

name: BookShark
description: Secular literature-based curriculum (sister company to Sonlight) with comprehensive all-subject packages and a 4-day schedule
notes: Secular alternative to Sonlight. All-Subject Packages cover language arts, science, math, history, and geography. 4-day/36-week schedule. Students read ~50 books per year. Does not cover high school fully (ends around age 16/level J). Heavy reading load requires parent involvement for read-alouds. Books reusable for siblings.

name: Timberdoodle
description: Hands-on, thinking-skills-focused curriculum kits curated from hundreds of publishers, available in Christian and non-religious editions
notes: Available in three tiers: Basic ($395-580, core subjects only), Complete ($800-990, all subjects), and Elite (premium extras). Both Classic (Christian) and Non-Religious editions at same price. Strong emphasis on STEM, logic puzzles, and thinking skills. Kit Customizer tool lets you swap items before purchasing. Does not include foreign language or literature book sets. Emphasizes independent learning as early as possible.

name: The Good and the Beautiful
description: Affordable, beautifully designed Christian curriculum covering all subjects with integrated family-style history and science units
notes: Exceptionally affordable — Language Arts levels 1-5 available as FREE PDF downloads. Courses typically $10-125 each. History and science are family-style (teach multiple ages together). Language Arts integrates multiple subjects (reading, spelling, grammar, writing, art, geography). Faith content is general Christian worldview, not denomination-specific. Can feel advanced for listed grade level — take placement test. Some reviewers note limited depth and US-centric content.

name: My Father's World
description: Christian curriculum blending Charlotte Mason, classical education, and unit studies with a global missions perspective
notes: Teaches multiple children together via Family Learning Cycle (grades 2-8 learn same history/science, different depth). History is the backbone with other subjects integrated around it. Math and language arts are separate purchases from other publishers (e.g., Spelling Power, Saxon). Bible and Christian values woven into every subject with a global missions emphasis. 4-day schedule. Lots of reading required. Cathy Duffy Top Pick.

name: Oak Meadow
description: Secular Waldorf-inspired curriculum emphasizing creativity, nature, and experiential learning with optional accredited enrollment
notes: Founded 1975 by Waldorf-trained teacher. Covers all subjects including art and music. K-3 coursebooks include all subjects; grades 5-8 have separate coursebooks per subject; high school uses Saxon Math and texts from secular publishers. Optional accredited distance learning school ($1500-2500/year) with teacher evaluations and transcripts. Gentle pacing in early years with increasingly rigorous academics later. May frustrate gifted/fast learners. Heavy on written responses and long-form assignments.

name: Gather 'Round Homeschool
description: Charlotte Mason-inspired Christian unit study curriculum designed for teaching all ages together with minimal prep
notes: Covers all subjects EXCEPT math (separate math program now available). One purchase works for ALL children in the family. Units are 20 lessons (~4-5 weeks each), organized in a 4-year cycle. Covers grammar, spelling, writing, art, reading, comprehension, geography, science, social studies, history, and Bible. Digital or print options. Not accredited. Cost can add up for single-child families but is very economical for large families. High school science coverage may need supplementation.

name: Calvert Homeschool
description: Secular, structured curriculum with 125+ years of history, offering print-based K-2 and online courses for grades 3-12
notes: Founded 1897. Print-based for K-2, online platform for grades 3-12 with 45+ courses, videos, and interactive exercises. 160 lessons + 20 review lessons per course with built-in assessments. Optional Calvert Academy accreditation ($1500-3200/year) with NCAA approval and recognized diplomas. Mastery-based approach with step-by-step lesson plans. Only 5-15 minutes daily parent prep. Subjects are thoughtfully cross-referenced (e.g., Civil War history paired with Whitman poetry in LA).

name: Master Books
description: Affordable, open-and-go Christian curriculum with Charlotte Mason-inspired short lessons and a strong biblical worldview
notes: Very affordable for a complete curriculum. Four-subject bundles available (math, language arts, history, science). 180 daily lessons per subject, ~1 page per day. Biblical worldview deeply integrated into every subject — cannot be separated from content. Does not align with Common Core. Some parents find lessons too easy/gentle. Optional Master Books Academy online videos available for self-paced instruction. Good for special needs and reluctant learners due to gentle approach.

name: Moving Beyond the Page
description: Secular, literature-based integrated curriculum designed for gifted and creative learners with interdisciplinary unit studies
notes: Organized by age range rather than grade (ages 4-5 through 12-14). 3-week interdisciplinary units combining language arts, science, and social studies around central themes. Math curriculum available for some levels but not all — may need to supplement. Hands-on and project-based with dioramas, cooking, experiments, essays. Online version allows multiple siblings at no extra cost. Used by 100+ accredited distance learning schools. Particularly strong for gifted learners. 4-day schedule. Ages 9+ become increasingly independent.

name: Daily Wonder
description: Waldorf-rooted homeschool curriculum created by three experienced Waldorf teachers, integrating all academic subjects through story, hands-on activities, movement, music, and visual/practical arts in daily morning lesson plans
notes: Created by Canadian Waldorf teachers who built and ran a Waldorf school. Digital PDF curriculum with 10 units per year (15 lesson days + 5 flex days each). Each day includes opening verse, movement, math, recall/review, new material, bookwork, story, and closing verse. Daily time commitment is 1-2 hours. Includes access to Wonder Hub with resource videos and supportive community. Individual units available at $44 each to trial. Also offers supplementary ukulele, recorder, pentatonic flute, and handwork courses. Aligned to BC Ministry of Education learning standards (translatable to other provinces/states). Block-teaching model typical of Waldorf education.

name: Earthschooling
description: Award-winning, secular Waldorf curriculum integrating nature studies, storytelling, painting, handwork, Eurythmy, and academics through hands-on activities and natural rhythms, with over 100 hours of teacher support tutorials
notes: The first online Waldorf curriculum provider, operating since 1994. Three purchase tiers: Core Curriculum (PDFs/images/MP3s), Curriculum Package (adds teacher video tutorials for crafts, watercolor, chalk drawing), and Living Lessons (adds video of Waldorf teacher teaching your student). Kindergarten package is $200; upper grades run $250-325. Lifetime Family Package (PreK-G8) is $2,225 without Living Lessons or $4,500 with them. High school (G9-12) is a separate $750 add-on converted to Waldorf School Online. Includes Eurythmy (unique among homeschool curricula), French lessons, music, international/multi-cultural content. Provides alternate lesson blocks for cultural sensitivity (e.g., secular alternatives to Saints block). 30% discount for AU/CA/NZ families. Scholarships available (up to 50% off). Lifetime access includes all future updates. Weekly Q&A sessions with Waldorf teacher included. Accepted by universities for transcripts.

name: Torchlight Curriculum
description: Literature-based, secular humanist curriculum emphasizing diverse authors, Socratic discussion, critical thinking, and hands-on projects across language arts, social studies, science, art, and music
notes: Covers Pre-K through Level 5 (ages 4-14) plus individual Constellation Studies for older students. Does NOT include math or explicit reading/phonics instruction (after Pre-K) — families must supplement. PDF-only curriculum; books purchased separately or borrowed from library. Level Pre-K is $32; Levels K-2 are $40-42; Level 3 is $47; Level 4 is $47; Level 5 is $52 ($76 with art and finance bundle). 36 weeks of daily lessons per level. Strong emphasis on diverse literature and multicultural perspectives. Socratic-inspired discussion and inquiry-based learning throughout. Includes art, music, philosophy, game logic, and creative writing (NaNoWriMo novel project in Levels 4-5). Cathy Duffy Top 103 Pick. Explicitly secular humanist — addresses worldview topics from humanist perspective, not religiously neutral. Heavy reading load; significant parent involvement required for discussions and activities. Very affordable curriculum guide, but book costs can add $100-200 per level if not using library.
